code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test10-JAN-2024Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, which may indicate possible diabetic nephropathy or another renal pathology. Urine is brown, cloudy, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Low specific gravity may reflect impaired concentrating ability. Hemoglobin A1c is unexpectedly low, possibly due to anemia or recent blood loss/transfusion. Recommend further renal and metabolic evaluation and clinical correlation.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
